results.alt: A desktop device that could extract a specific memory from a person's mind and render it as a physical, three-dimensional object — a crystalline sculpture that, when held, allowed anyone to experience that memory as if it were their own. Recalled after users began preferring printed memories to real life. Memory Printer

Recalled after users began preferring printed memories to real life. Memory Printer

The Gravity Umbrella was a revolutionary personal device that generated a localized anti-gravity field within a 2-meter radius of its canopy. By inverting the gravitational constant in a contained bubble, the user could float, glide, or ascend at will — simply by tilting the handle. Dr. Voss reportedly tested it above the Swiss Alps before the prototype vanished during a thunderstorm. Gravity Umbrella

A bedside device that recorded dreams in real-time during REM sleep and played them back as full-color, immersive video the following morning. Discontinued after users discovered their dreams were being used to train advertising algorithms. Dream Recorder

A city-block-sized apparatus that could synthesize any weather condition on demand — from gentle spring rain to category-5 hurricanes — within a 50-kilometer radius. The Nimbus Collective built three units before governments worldwide banned the technology after the Great Fog Incident of 2141. Weather Generator

A handheld compass that, instead of pointing north, always pointed in the direction of the nearest source of genuine human joy — a laughing child, a couple in love, a person achieving a lifelong dream. Designed to combat urban loneliness. Emotion Compass

A telescope that, rather than looking across space, looked across time — allowing the observer to view any moment in history or glimpse probable futures. The Anachronist Guild claimed to have built one, but their headquarters was found empty one morning, with no trace of when they had left. Time Telescope

Step inside the world's only museum dedicated to fictional inventions —
            gravity umbrellas, weather generators, memory printers, and more.
            These devices never existed. But they should have. Inventions That Never Were
